title: calyptrostegia cornucopiae
intro: Calyptrostegia cornucopiae, also known as the wright's hibiscus or the hibiscus bracteatus, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, characterized by its showy and fragrant flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Calyptrostegia cornucopiae include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
